note: First systematic WA outreach. 11 campaigns across 5 brokers, 2,362 contacts. Main issue: 66.3% failed rate across the entire launch.

failed_error_codes.label: Not registered on WhatsApp
failed_error_codes.description: Number exists but not registered on WhatsApp. UAE owners often use local SIMs without WA.

failed_error_codes.label: Throttling — sending limit exceeded
failed_error_codes.description: Meta restricts marketing templates when volume is too high.

failed_error_codes.label: Low template quality rating
failed_error_codes.description: Previous campaigns received complaints — Meta reduces delivery rate.

templates.name: Standard Template
templates.hook: None — starts with 'Hi, I hope you are doing well...'
issues: Opening line immediately identifies as cold outreach
Too long — takes over 30 seconds to read
No specificity in first sentence
Weak CTA at the end

templates.name: Short Template
templates.hook: A property like yours came up today
templates.cta: Would you be open to hearing an offer?

baseline_kpi.note: First launch — fixed as benchmark for future campaigns

campaigns.status_note: Worst delivery among all campaigns. List needs verification before next send.
strengths: Reply rate 6.1% — above average among those who read
issues: Failed 61% — majority of base unreachable

campaigns.status_note: Largest absolute failed count (504), but best read rate (42%) and 2nd best reply rate (9.5%). Quality of reached contacts is very high.
strengths: Read rate 42% — best among all campaigns
22 replies — most leads from a single campaign
issues: 504 failed — absolute maximum losses. Priority #1 to investigate.

campaigns.status_note: 74% failed — 3 out of 4 messages not delivered. Sidra owners likely non-residents with foreign numbers.
issues: Worst failed percentage — 74%
Reply rate 2.6% — template may not resonate with Sidra audience

campaigns.status_note: Best delivery rate among Sidra campaigns. But reply rate 1.3% — lowest of all. Good technical execution, poor template conversion.
strengths: Delivery rate 57.4% — best among Sidra campaigns
issues: Reply rate 1.3% — lowest of all campaigns with replies
1 opt-out
Standard template underperforming for this audience

campaigns.status_note: Short template works — 1 in 8 readers replied. Failed rate typical for premium projects with non-resident owners.
strengths: Reply rate 4.4% — strong result for cold outreach
issues: Failed 63.8% — premium project owners often have foreign numbers

campaigns.status_note: Sample too small (29 contacts) for any statistical conclusions. Results not representative.
issues: Base too small — minimum 200+ contacts needed
0 replies
No conclusions possible at this sample size

campaigns.status_note: Decent reply rate for short template. Read rate low at 24% — test different send times.
strengths: Reply rate 3.8% — solid for cold outreach
issues: Read rate 24% — lowest among Alikhan campaigns

campaigns.status_note: CRITICAL: 82.3% failed — record worst delivery. Only 22 of 130 received the message. Base likely sourced from low-quality data.
strengths: Reply rate 4.5% from those who read — content works
issues: Failed 82.3% — absolute delivery failure record
Base requires complete replacement and verification

campaigns.status_note: Average campaign. Delivery above average for Lev. Growth potential through improved template hook.
strengths: Delivery rate 41% — best among Lev campaigns
issues: Read rate 23% — only 1 in 4 recipients opened

campaigns.status_note: Worst overall campaign by combined metrics. Three red flags simultaneously: read rate 10%, 0 replies, failed 62.5%.
issues: Read rate 10% — worst among all campaigns
0 replies
Failed 62.5%
Full rethink required: base, template, send time

campaigns.status_note: THE PARADOX: worst delivery (84.6% failed) but best conversion. Read rate 51% and reply rate 10.2% are absolute records. Fix the base — this is the most profitable campaign.
strengths: Read rate 51% — RECORD — every 2nd delivered message opened
Reply rate 10.2% — BEST of all campaigns
Rency shows best conversion among all brokers
issues: Failed 84.6% — together with Arabian Ranches: delivery catastrophe
Only 49 of 338 received the message

brokers_summary.name: Dmitrii
brokers_summary.note: Highest reply count (25) but also largest failed volume (666). Maple Average is a star — Arabian Ranches needs urgent base replacement.

brokers_summary.name: Alikhan
brokers_summary.note: Short template user — consistent results across premium properties. Como needs bigger base. Scale short template to other brokers.

brokers_summary.name: Rency
brokers_summary.note: BEST CONVERSION of all brokers. 51% read rate and 10.2% reply rate are records. Critical priority: fix base quality. If delivery reaches 70% — this becomes the top campaign.

brokers_summary.name: Vladimir
brokers_summary.note: 74% failed — Sidra base likely contains many non-residents with foreign numbers. Needs full list verification.

brokers_summary.name: Hicham
brokers_summary.note: Best delivery rate for Sidra (57.4%). Sender number performs well technically. Reply rate 1.3% — template issue, not technical. Use Hicham's number for next Sidra wave.

brokers_summary.name: Lev
brokers_summary.note: Primo is the worst campaign overall — requires complete rethink. Grande shows potential. Switch both to short template for next launch.

key_findings.text: 66.3% of all contacts (1,567 of 2,362) failed to receive the message. The real campaign scale was 3x smaller than potential. Database verification before next launch is the #1 priority.
ACT Towers (Rency) is the campaign paradox: worst delivery (84.6% failed) but absolute records in read rate (51%) and reply rate (10.2%). Fix the base — this is the most valuable campaign.
Short template (Royal Atlantis, Viceroy, ACT Towers) consistently outperforms standard. Reply rates: 4.4%, 3.8%, 10.2% vs standard template campaigns averaging 2–4%. Scale immediately to all brokers.

Despite 504 failed contacts, Maple Average delivered the highest read rate (42%) and 22 replies — most leads of any single campaign. Quality of reached contacts is exceptional. Investigate failed causes urgently.

Both Sidra campaigns combined: 74% and 38% failed rates, reply rates of 2.6% and 1.3%. Owners are likely non-residents with foreign SIMs. Standard template not working. Hicham's sender number performs best technically.
Arabian Ranches: 82.3% failed. Primo: 62.5% failed + 10% read rate + 0 replies. Both require complete base replacement and template switch before any future launch.

$44 total spend. Actual CPL: $1.07 (41 total replies). Qualified CPL: $2.44 (18 interested replies). Extremely cost-efficient if base quality is fixed — same budget with 70% delivery = 3x more contacts reached.

recommendations: PRIORITY 1 — Verify all databases before next launch using WA Checker, PhoneBook or WAAPI. Focus on ACT Towers, Arabian Ranches, Primo bases first.
PRIORITY 2 — Switch ALL campaigns to short template. Standard template is underperforming across every broker using it. No exceptions.
Segment sends by 200–300 contacts per day per broker to reduce Meta throttling (error 131049).
Retry throttled contacts (error 131049) after exactly 48 hours — these are reachable contacts that were lost to rate limiting.
Scale Rency's ACT Towers approach: same short template + verified base = potential 10%+ reply rate at scale.
Use Hicham's sender number (8296) for all future Sidra campaigns — 57.4% delivery vs Vladimir's 24.7% on same audience.
Expand Como Residences base to minimum 200 contacts before next launch — current 29 contacts give no statistical validity.
Test send times: Tuesday–Thursday, 10:00–12:00 or 19:00–21:00 Dubai time. Current send time unknown — this is a quick win.
Add error code tracking per campaign to distinguish 131026 (no WA) vs 131049 (throttle) vs 131047 (template quality) failures.
Track reply quality: separate 'interested' from 'opt-out' and 'wrong number' responses. Current 41 replies include unknown quality mix.
Build Bitrix24 integration to track: reply → meeting → deal. Without this, true campaign ROI is unmeasurable.
